structured_case.title: Nguyen v. Summit Logistics — promissory estoppel after relocation reliance
relief_sought: Reliance damages of $48,200 covering relocation, lease termination, and lost prior compensation
Six months of promised base salary as reasonable reliance equivalent
key_facts: On August 11, 2025 defendant's VP of Operations sent plaintiff a signed written offer for a Senior Analyst role starting September 15, 2025.
In reliance, plaintiff resigned her Texas position, broke her Dallas apartment lease, and relocated her family to Fayetteville.
On September 12, 2025 defendant rescinded the offer citing an internal hiring freeze with no severance or relocation reimbursement.
No formal employment contract was countersigned, but the offer letter set salary, start date, and duties with specificity.

contract_clauses.name: Offer terms
contract_clauses.text: We are pleased to offer you the position of Senior Analyst at an annual base salary of $115,000, beginning September 15, 2025. This offer is contingent on standard background screening.

cases.name: Bryant v. River Valley Mills
cases.holding_summary: Promissory estoppel may enforce an employer's clear promise where the employee reasonably relies to detriment and injustice can be avoided only by enforcement.

cases.name: Holloway v. Arkansas Telecom
cases.holding_summary: Reliance damages are recoverable where promissory estoppel applies; expectation damages may follow only when the promise is definite and supported by a writing.

cases.name: Davis v. Ozark Manufacturing
cases.holding_summary: Termination without cause during a definite employment term breaches the contract; damages are measured by wages for the unexpired term minus mitigation earnings.

cases.name: Miller v. Pioneer Investments
cases.holding_summary: Fraud in the inducement requires a material misrepresentation, scienter, reasonable reliance, and resulting damages.

favorable_precedents.name: Bryant v. River Valley Mills
favorable_precedents.holding_summary: Promissory estoppel may enforce an employer's clear promise where the employee reasonably relies to detriment and injustice can be avoided only by enforcement.

unfavorable_precedents.name: Holloway v. Arkansas Telecom
unfavorable_precedents.holding_summary: Reliance damages are recoverable where promissory estoppel applies; expectation damages may follow only when the promise is definite and supported by a writing.

predicted_outcomes.methodology: Bayesian-style estimate from CAP precedent sample plaintiff outcomes, adjusted for party role and precedent depth.

scenarios.description: Liability finding with reliance damages of $48,200 awarded.
Reliance plus partial expectation award (3-6 months wages).
Defense verdict on at-will doctrine and lack of definite promise.

predicted_outcomes.likely_judgment_summary: Liability finding with reliance damages of $48,200 awarded.

win_strategies.title: Anchor the clear-promise element to the signed offer letter
actions: Plead the August 11, 2025 offer verbatim with role, salary, and start date.
Lead with Bryant v. River Valley Mills as Arkansas authority.
win_strategies.rationale: Definiteness is the gateway element; the written offer satisfies it.

win_strategies.title: Build the reliance and detriment narrative
actions: Attach the resignation acceptance, lease-termination receipt, and relocation invoices.
Sequence each act in time relative to the offer and rescission.
win_strategies.rationale: Reliance must be reasonable and detrimental; documentary sequencing crystallizes the chain.

win_strategies.title: Frame damages as reliance-equivalent
actions: Quantify out-of-pocket costs ($48,200) plus the value of the forfeited Texas role.
Argue six months' salary as a reasonable reliance proxy under Holloway.
win_strategies.rationale: Reliance damages are the most defensible measure; expectation damages remain a stretch.

win_strategies.title: Plead negligent misrepresentation in the alternative
actions: Allege the VP's representations were made without reasonable basis given the impending hiring freeze.
Preserve discovery into internal communications around the freeze decision.
win_strategies.rationale: Alternative theory preserves recovery if the court treats the offer as an at-will promise only.

win_strategies.title: Position for early settlement
actions: Send a demand letter quantifying reliance damages with supporting receipts.
Offer mediation before discovery to capture defendant's defense-cost calculus.
win_strategies.rationale: Reliance-damage cases frequently resolve once documented out-of-pocket loss is undisputed.

favorable_judgment_tactics.detail: Move for partial summary judgment on the existence of a clear promise once the offer letter is authenticated.
Anchor the case to one offer letter, one rescission, and a short receipt list.
Lead with reliance damages; reserve partial expectation as alternative.
Use receipts-based demand to drive resolution above out-of-pocket but below full expectation.

complaint_outline.content: Linh Nguyen v. Summit Logistics Corp.
Plaintiff: Linh Nguyen; Defendant: Summit Logistics Corp.
promissory estoppel; negligent misrepresentation; alternative breach of implied contract
Numbered paragraphs tracing the offer, plaintiff's reliance acts, and the September 12 rescission.
Reliance damages of $48,200; six months of promised base salary; costs of suit

proposed_exhibits.description: Plaintiff's resignation letter to prior employer
Dallas lease termination receipt and relocation invoices
Key precedent: 260 Ark. 510 (1976) Bryant v. River Valley Mills

pre_filing_checklist: Confirm three-year statute of limitations for promissory estoppel claims.
Verify filing fee and e-filing account for Washington County Circuit Court.
Attach civil cover sheet if required by local rules.
Review formatting requirements (margins, line numbering, word limits).

steps: Open e-filing portal for Circuit Court.
Upload PDF with embedded exhibits; pay filing fee.
Serve defendant per Rule 4 / local equivalent within required period.
how_to_file.note: This system does not e-file on your behalf.

top_precedents.name: Bryant v. River Valley Mills
top_precedents.headnotes: Promissory estoppel may enforce an employer's clear promise where the employee reasonably relies to detriment and injustice can be avoided only by enforcement.
top_precedents.snippet: Plaintiff sold her prior business in reliance on the offered position; the court enforced the promise to the extent of reliance damages.

top_precedents.name: Holloway v. Arkansas Telecom
top_precedents.headnotes: Reliance damages are recoverable where promissory estoppel applies; expectation damages may follow only when the promise is definite and supported by a writing.
top_precedents.snippet: Recovery limited to documented reliance; expectation damages denied for indefinite promise.
